title: Unnecessary using should be removed
description: Unnecessary using directives refer to importing namespaces, types or creating aliases that are not used or referenced anywhere in the code. Although they don’t affect the runtime behavior of the application after compilation, removing them will: Improve the readability and maintainability of the code. Help avoid potential naming conflicts.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

logic.detection_conditions: Find using directives for namespaces not referenced in the file

conditions: Namespace not referenced anywhere in code

logic.reasoning: Unused using directives clutter code and can cause naming conflicts. They should be removed.

should_trigger: using System; // when System types not used
should_not_trigger: using System.Collections.Generic; // when List<T> is used
